title: Karma Yoga

principle.text:
Karmany evadhikaras te ma phaleshu kadachana.

You have the right to action alone, never to its fruits.

This is the central instruction of the Gita and the seed of karma yoga: do the work without keeping one eye on what the work will pay back. The PM title, the promotion conversation, the save that would prove your judgment right -- these are not yours to control. The email you write tonight, the question you bring into tomorrow's call, the one-pager no one asked for -- those are entirely yours.

For someone moving from CSM to PM, attachment to outcome is not laziness. It is usually fear dressed as strategy: wanting to know the move will work before making it. But the verse is precise. The right to action, not to fruit. The practice is in the doing. Ship the work. The fruit will follow or it won't, and neither defines what the work was worth.

move: Read 'Lessons from Stripe' by Daniel Gross (it is easy to find via search). Spend 15 minutes with it. Notice where the builders moved without waiting to know it would work -- and where the waiting would have killed the thing.
question: Where in your current work are you waiting for a signal before beginning something you already know needs doing?
